title: Venezuelan Plantain Empanadas
description: A traditional Venezuelan dish, these empanadas are filled with the sweetness of plantains and the crunch of fried pastry. Perfect as a snack or dessert.
instructions: Preheat oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (180°C).
Peel and slice the plantains into 1-inch pieces.
In a bowl, mix together the sliced plantains and a pinch of salt.
Roll out the empanada dough and cut out circles of dough.
Place a spoonful of the plantain mixture onto one half of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with x.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Fry the empanadas for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Drain the empanadas on paper towels and serve warm.
